posts.post_text:
A single unrestricted API key cost one team €54,000 in 13 hours.

Not a breach. Not an attack. A Firebase browser key left without domain restrictions, quietly racking up Gemini API calls from bots scraping their public endpoint.

The fix took 30 seconds — adding a domain allowlist. The bill took months to resolve.

This is the part of cloud architecture nobody talks about: the cost of defaults. Every managed service ships with permissive defaults because restrictive ones generate support tickets. But permissive defaults at scale are financial time bombs.

Three things I now check on every project:
1. API key restrictions (domain, IP, or app-level)
2. Budget alerts at 50%, 80%, and 100% of expected spend
3. Kill switches — automated spend caps that shut down endpoints

The best security practice is also the best cost practice.

#CloudSecurity #DevOps

hook_variants.label: A (original)
hook_variants.text: A single unrestricted API key cost one team €54,000 in 13 hours.

hook_variants.label: B
hook_variants.text: Your cloud bill has a ticking time bomb in it — and it's probably an API key you forgot about.

hook_variants.label: C
hook_variants.text: I used to think cloud cost overruns were a big-company problem. Then I saw a €54K bill from a weekend project.

posts.source_title: €54k spike in 13h from unrestricted Firebase browser key

posts.post_text:
Vector databases are the most over-engineered part of most AI agent stacks.

For 90% of use cases — chatbots, personal assistants, small-scale RAG — you don't need Pinecone, Weaviate, or Chroma. You need SQLite and a clear schema.

memweave just proved this. It's an open-source agent memory system that uses Markdown files + SQLite. No vector database. No embedding pipeline. No infrastructure to maintain.

The results: faster queries, simpler debugging, and zero cold-start latency.

We've collectively confused 'sophisticated' with 'effective.' A hash map lookup on structured metadata is often faster AND more accurate than approximate nearest neighbor search on 1536-dimensional embeddings.

Not every nail needs the vector-shaped hammer.

#AI #OpenSource
